summary: Clean soundboard recording with a slightly odd mix, but the show shines thanks to stellar Dark Star, Good Lovin' jam and emotive performances throughout.
review: Fans rave about this 1970 gem – Jerry’s soulful lead on Dark Star and High Time, Bob Weir’s inspired jams in Good Lovin' and Cold Rain & Snow, and a seamless flow that stitches together High Time → Dark Star → The Other One → St. Stephen → NFA → Lovelight → Me and My Uncle. The soundboard source is generally clean, though the stereo split (instruments on one side, vocals on the other) feels unusual.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high star ratings (mostly 4‑5), strong praise for musical performance and only minor comments about a quirky mix, indicating an excellent show with good recording quality.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clean overall sound but unusual stereo separation (most instruments on one channel, vocals on the other) and a brief cut of the opening Morning Dew.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The concert is praised for its inventive segues – High Time flowing into a spacey Dark Star, which morphs into The Other One, then a crisp St. Stephen → NFA transition leading to an ecstatic Lovelight that beautifully leads into Me and My Uncle.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for soulful, soaring lead work on Dark Star, High Time and the overall emotional intensity of the set.
band_member_comments.Bob: Highlighted for his standout jam in Good Lovin' and beautiful phrasing on Cold Rain & Snow.
band_member_comments.Drums: Described as jazzy and tight; drum breaks into St. Stephen and NFA are noted as particularly effective.
